key_insight: In an evolutionary multi-agent market simulator, different mechanisms act as mostly separable control knobs: selection mainly affects strategy diversity, price formation mainly affects realism, and behavioral bias mainly affects fragility.
engineering_relevance: This decomposition helps model builders tune agent-based market systems more deliberately by choosing which mechanism to change based on the emergent property they want to improve or stress-test.
method: Built a coevolving, endogenous-price ABM with 120 heterogeneous agents.
Made four mechanisms pluggable and tested matched 3x20-seed interventions.
Compared outcomes across diversity, realism, and fragility metrics using significance tests and confidence intervals.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports strong, statistically supported effects such as increased entropy under quality-diversity selection, higher realism with reflexive price feedback, and larger fragility under stronger behavioral bias, while consensus topology showed no robust effect.
watch_item: Results come from single-mechanism sweeps in a specific simulator, so the apparent independence of mechanisms may not fully generalize to other market models or coupled interventions.
paper_title: Decomposing Financial Market Dynamics via Mechanism Analysis in an Evolutionary Multi-Agent Simulation
